subject: fix(boot): decouple DebugPill from App and add zero-dependency error overlay
body:
The blank white screen with no debug pill happens because main.tsx
statically imports both App and DebugPill. If any module in App's deep
dependency tree (react-router, supabase, pages, hooks) throws during
evaluation, the entire ES module fails and DebugPill never mounts —
leaving the user with zero diagnostic information.

Three changes to make the boot process resilient:

1. Dynamic import for App — DebugPill now mounts first via static import
   (tiny chain: React + debugLog). App loads asynchronously via import().
   If App's chunk fails, DebugPill still renders and shows the error.

2. Inline error overlay in index.html — a classic (non-module) script
   that catches errors and shows them on screen before any React code
   runs. Also includes a 10-second boot timeout to catch silent failures.

3. Boot-stage logging — debugLog calls at each boot stage (main.tsx
   executing, DebugPill mounted, App loading, App rendered, Supabase
   config status) so the debug pill shows exactly where things went wrong.

Tags: boot, debug, resilience, blank-screen
Complexity: 3
Urgency: 5
Impact: user-facing
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: blank-screen-fix
Semver: patch

title: decouple DebugPill from App and add zero-dependency error overlay
